req.title: Hr-Admin deletes policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a policy-document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an hr-admin can delete a policy-document via the UI, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and a 200/201 response on success. Error paths cover 400 (validation), 401 (unauthenticated), and 403 (forbidden role). I selected: (1) EP positive – valid hr-admin deletes an existing document and audit record is verified; (2) NEG negative – unauthenticated user is rejected with 401; (3) DT negative – authenticated user with insufficient role (viewer) receives 403; (4) BVA boundary – deletion attempted on a document whose ID is at the minimum valid boundary (ID=1); (5) STC state-transition – document transitions from 'active' to 'deleted' state and cannot be retrieved afterwards.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_policy_document_success
preconditions: A policy-document with known ID exists in state 'active'
actions: Select the target policy-document by ID
Click the Delete button and confirm the dialog
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 response containing the deleted entity ID
Policy-document no longer appears in the listing
An audit record for the delete action is written
trace: R-WEB-055: positive path – valid hr-admin deletes document
R-WEB-055: audit record must be written on success
R-WEB-055: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_delete_policy_document_success(hr_admin_page, create_policy_document, audit_api_client):
    doc_id = create_policy_document(title="Test Policy EP")
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/policy-documents")
    page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{doc_id}'] >> text=Delete").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()
    response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/policy-documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(doc_id) in str(body), "Entity ID missing from response"
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{doc_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()
    audit_records = audit_api_client.get_records(entity_id=doc_id, action="delete")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_policy_document
preconditions: A policy-document with known ID exists
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_policy_document(base_url, create_policy_document, audit_api_client):
    doc_id = create_policy_document(title="Test Policy NEG-401")
    url = f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}"
    response = requests.delete(url, headers={}, cookies={})
    assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"
    audit_records = audit_api_client.get_records(entity_id=doc_id, action="delete")
    assert len(audit_records) == 0, "Audit record must not be written on 401"
    exists = <helper_fn>.document_exists(doc_id)
    assert exists, "Document should still exist after rejected delete"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_policy_document
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'viewer' (not hr-admin)
A policy-document with known ID exists
trace: R-WEB-055: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_policy_document(base_url, viewer_auth_headers, create_policy_document, audit_api_client):
    doc_id = create_policy_document(title="Test Policy DT-403")
    url = f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}"
    response = requests.delete(url, headers=viewer_auth_headers)
    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"
    audit_records = audit_api_client.get_records(entity_id=doc_id, action="delete")
    assert len(audit_records) == 0, "Audit record must not be written on 403"
    exists = <helper_fn>.document_exists(doc_id)
    assert exists, "Document should still exist after forbidden delete"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_policy_document_minimum_boundary_id
preconditions: A policy-document exists whose ID equals the minimum valid boundary value (ID=1)
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 response containing entity ID=1
Document with ID=1 no longer appears in listing
An audit record is written for ID=1
trace: R-WEB-055: BVA on document ID lower boundary
R-WEB-055: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MIN_DOC_ID = 1

def test_hr_admin_delete_policy_document_minimum_boundary_id(hr_admin_page, ensure_document_with_id, audit_api_client):
    ensure_document_with_id(MIN_DOC_ID, title="BVA Min ID Policy")
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/policy-documents")
    page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{MIN_DOC_ID}'] >> text=Delete").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()
    response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/policy-documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(MIN_DOC_ID) in str(body), "Min boundary entity ID missing from response"
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{MIN_DOC_ID}']")).not_to_be_visible()
    audit_records = audit_api_client.get_records(entity_id=MIN_DOC_ID, action="delete")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record not written for boundary ID"

tests.name: test_deleted_policy_document_transitions_to_deleted_state
actions: Record document state as 'active' via GET /policy-documents/{doc_id}
Navigate to /policy-documents and delete the document via the UI form
expected: After deletion: GET /policy-documents/{doc_id} returns 404 or state='deleted'
State transition active -> deleted is confirmed
trace: R-WEB-055: policy-document is deleted after action
R-WEB-055: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_deleted_policy_document_transitions_to_deleted_state(hr_admin_page, create_policy_document, base_url, hr_admin_auth_headers, audit_api_client):
    doc_id = create_policy_document(title="STC State Transition Policy")
    pre = requests.get(f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}", headers=hr_admin_auth_headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json().get("state") == "active"
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/policy-documents")
    page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{doc_id}'] >> text=Delete").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()
    del_resp = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/policy-documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert del_resp.status in (200, 201)
    post = requests.get(f"{base_url}/policy-documents/{doc_id}", headers=hr_admin_auth_headers)
    assert post.status_code == 404 or post.json().get("state") == "deleted"
    audit_records = audit_api_client.get_records(entity_id=doc_id, action="delete")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record not written on state transition"
